question: What is the current Fed funds rate?
draftAnswer: ## Result Summary Internal Corpus Answer: unavailable in current local corpus. The local documents contain only Acme Corporation financial and compliance information, not Federal Reserve policy data. External Context: available. - [www.fedprimerate.com](https://www.fedprimerate.com/fedfundsrate/federal_funds_rate_history.htm) | Fed Funds Target Rate History | December 10, 2025: 3.50 - 3.75 (The Current Target Range for the Fed Funds Rate) - [www.nerdwallet.com](https://www.nerdwallet.com/banking/news/what-is-the-fed-rate) | What Is the Federal Funds Rate? - NerdWallet | Right now, the Fed interest rate is 3.50% to 3.75%. The FOMC set the rate at its Dec. 9-10 meeting. - [www.frbdiscountwindow.org](https://www.frbdiscountwindow.org) | The Federal Reserve Discount Window | The Federal Open Market Committee decided to maintain the target federal funds rate at 3.50 to 3.75 percent. FOMC Statement. January 28, 2026. - [tradingeconomics.com](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/interest-rate) | United States Fed Funds Interest Rate - Trading Economics | The benchmark interest rate in the {ADDRESS} was last recorded at 3.75 percent.
